prevalence.value: 25-45% (≥5 medications among older adults; varies by setting)
prevalence.trend: increasing with multimorbidity and chronic disease treatment

vulnerablePopulations: older adults with limited access to medication review
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Medication count with assessment for inappropriate meds and adverse effects; reconciliation is key

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of millions of older adults
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; structured medication review is limited

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often discovered after adverse events; limited pharmacist involvement
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Medication reconciliation, deprescribing, simplify regimens, avoid high-risk sedatives
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: OTC use, fragmented care, and limited follow-up
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Community programs and family support can aid adherence and reconciliation
